# Current Overhaul Phase

## Status: IN_PROGRESS
**Active Phase:** Phase 11: Gameplay Audit & MDA Deep-Dive

## Summary
Phase 11 is active. This phase is focused on gathering high-fidelity telemetry, decision-making logs, and qualitative "feel" data through extended play sessions using `agent-browser` and specialized Player Personas (Safe Grinder, High-Risk Scholar, Street Hustler, AI Control), while hardening the audit workflow so authoritative checkpoints survive browser-session loss.

## Objective
Identify remaining balance wrinkles, broken math, and opportunities for systemic depth before committing to Phase 12 coding tasks.

## Next Steps
- [x] Task 1: Setup Audit Infrastructure (Configure `agent-browser` and logging templates).
- [ ] Task 2: Execute "The Safe Grinder" Run (Persona A).
- [ ] Task 3: Execute "The High-Risk Scholar" Run (Persona B).
- [ ] Task 4: Execute "The Street Hustler" Run (Persona C).
- [ ] Task 5: Comparative AI Analysis (Persona D).
- [ ] Task 6: Synthesis & "Wrinkle" Report.

## Progress Notes
- 2026-03-24: Persona A Week 2 is now authoritative from the live `phase11-safe-grinder` continuity session. The slice did not reopen on the stale Week 1 boundary from the docs; it resumed mid-Week 2 at Labor Sector (`₡340 / Hunger 20 / Sanity 45 / Time 4CH`) with no active `Transit Strike` / `Sore Legs` condition. `Work Shift` remained a click-without-mutation surface, so the run fell back to Labor hustles; `Scrap Metal` ended the week but immediately triggered the hidden `Tetanus-Grade Laceration` event, converting the expected `+₡85` burst into a net `+₡5` before a forced `-5 Sanity / -2CH` patch-up choice. Week 2 stabilized at Turn 3 / `Hab-Pod 404` with `₡345 / Hunger 40 / Sanity 27 / Time 22CH`, and `scripts/lib/state-proxy.mjs` was patched to pass Linux `agent-browser batch` flags as `--args "--no-sandbox"` so post-action verification is reliable again.
- 2026-03-24: Implemented Phase 6: Gameplay & Automation Guardrails (Token Efficiency Plan). Updated `AGENTS.md`, `autonomous-runner-prompt.md`, and `agent-browser-recipes.json` to explicitly forbid "Hallucination Cheating" (Undo/Rewind) and enforce Modal-Awareness. Refactored `scripts/lib/state-proxy.mjs` to capture `activeChoiceContext` for better situational awareness. Verified the new guardrails by simulating a terminal burnout event and logging it as an "Audit Event."
- 2026-03-24: Implemented Phase 5: Tool Hygiene & Reliability.
 Eliminated "Tool Limping" by enforcing a strict "Command First, Flags Last" policy on Linux and mandating "Wait then Act" patterns in all browser recipes and skill instructions. Refactored `scripts/lib/state-proxy.mjs` and `scripts/cyberpunk-overhaul-phase11-checkpoint.mjs` to be compliant. Verified the new hygiene via a simulated Labor Sector job application check.
- 2026-03-22: Fixed GitHub issue `#12` on `main`. Investigation showed the remaining gap was runner-side, not app-side: `scripts/cyberpunk-overhaul-phase11-once.sh` compacted each browser recipe down to a single verification line, so fresh-context slices could still over-trust raw click success. The generated `startup-context.json` handoff now carries an explicit action-verification policy, preserves multiple verification lines for risky actions, and the runner prompt/docs now require before/after state checks for travel, Labor `Apply`, shopping, and `Work Shift` before the slice can continue. Validation: `bash -n scripts/cyberpunk-overhaul-phase11-once.sh` and `npm test -- tests/scripts/phase11-issues.test.ts`.
- 2026-03-22: Fixed GitHub issue `#11` on `main`. Investigation showed city travel automation was brittle because `CityScreen` rendered clickable `.bento-card` divs with no stable control semantics, forcing `agent-browser` toward text scans or targeted DOM eval. The city grid now renders semantic `button` controls with stable `city-travel-card-*` selectors and location metadata, the browser recipe now treats direct selector clicks as the baseline travel path, and the Phase 11 issue helper now recognizes `#11` as fixed baseline behavior when those selectors are recorded in the handoff docs. Validation: `npm test -- tests/ui/components/screens/CityScreen.test.ts tests/scripts/phase11-issues.test.ts` and `npx playwright test tests/e2e/locations.spec.ts tests/e2e/labor-sector-glitch.spec.ts --reporter=line`.
- 2026-03-22: Fixed GitHub issue `#10` on `main`. Investigation showed the Labor Sector `Apply` path was not a `GameState.applyForJob()` bug; the first job button sits just below the viewport in the dashboard, so a raw `agent-browser click` could report success without landing on an in-view pointer target. The Phase 11 browser recipe now treats job application as a scroll-and-verify flow, and `scripts/cyberpunk-overhaul-phase11-once.sh` now carries compact `preferred_actions` and bounded fallback guidance into runner context instead of only selectors plus one verification line. Validation: `bash -n scripts/cyberpunk-overhaul-phase11-once.sh`, a focused agent-browser repro showing raw click failure with unchanged `careerLevel = 0`, and a focused agent-browser repro showing `scrollintoview` plus direct click mutating `careerLevel = 1` / `CURRENT SHIFT = Sanitation-T3`.
- 2026-03-22: Extended the Phase 11 slice-issue ledger helper on `main` to cover issue identification. The CLI surface now includes `npm run p11:issues:identify`, which reads the bounded slice/handoff control surface, filters out issues already recorded as fixed baseline behavior, writes `slice-issue-ledger.md`, and generates the prompt for each remaining unresolved issue. The current version is heuristic and intentionally conservative: it handles known recurring Phase 11 issue classes rather than arbitrary natural-language extraction.
- 2026-03-22: Fixed GitHub issue `#9` on `main`. The Phase 11 continuity probe now treats visible onboarding as a reset state instead of trusting the pre-onboarding live `GameState` bridge, so `workflow:phase11:checkpoint:status` no longer reports `live_continuity` just because `window.__JONES_FASTLANE_SESSION__` exists on a fresh session. The status output now surfaces `onboarding_visible`, and `workflow:phase11:once` auto-restores the latest checkpoint for that case before gameplay. Treat older Week 8 notes that describe a false `live_continuity` report on the onboarding screen as historical pre-fix evidence. Validation: `npm test -- tests/scripts/phase11-checkpoint-session-utils.test.ts tests/scripts/phase11-checkpoint-utils.test.ts tests/services/LiveSessionBridge.test.ts` and a focused `workflow:phase11:checkpoint:status` run against an onboarding-visible session.
- 2026-03-22: Persona A Week 8 is now authoritative. The runner continuity artifact incorrectly reported `live_continuity`, but the named `phase11-safe-grinder` browser session had reopened on onboarding, so the slice first restored `docs/workflows/cyberpunk-overhaul/checkpoints/persona_a/week-07-save.json`. From the recovered Week 7 summary anchor at `₡286 / Debt ₡0 / Hunger 40% / Sanity 35 / Trauma Reboot 312h`, the live Week 8 build returned direct manual control, did not reproduce the old one-choice opener regression, and still allowed the full `Sanitation-T3` plus `Work Shift x3` labor route under `TRAUMA_REBOOT`. Week 8 closed at `₡458 / Debt ₡0 / Hunger 60% / Sanity 25` with `TRAUMA_REBOOT (266h)`, and the summary reconciled cleanly at `+₡172 / -10 sanity`.
- 2026-03-22: Fixed GitHub issue `#8` on `main`. `workflow:phase11:checkpoint:status` and `workflow:phase11:checkpoint:export` now probe the existing `agent-browser` page before any navigation, prefer a live `GameState` snapshot exposed through `window.__JONES_FASTLANE_SESSION__`, and fall back to `localStorage` only when no live bridge is available. The helper also waits for fresh navigations to settle and uses a larger browser-command buffer so full Phase 11 saves export reliably.
